theme: Review #101 — The office has soul but no memory. This review is about giving the space a sense of time, history, and earned personality.

ideas.title: Office Light Theme Parity
ideas.description: The office floor has no light-theme CSS overrides — switching to light mode makes the office nearly unreadable with dark backgrounds clashing against light chrome. Users who prefer light mode get a broken experience. Floor, desks, monitors, and plants all need light-mode custom properties.

ideas.title: Worker Task Progress Pulse on Desk Lamp
ideas.description: Desk lamps glow when working but they're static. The lamp brightness/pulse speed should reflect the worker's actual progress percentage — dim and slow at 10%, bright and fast at 90%. It creates a visceral 'getting closer' feeling without needing to read numbers.

ideas.title: Conference Table Meeting Topic Display
ideas.description: When workers huddle at the conference table, there's a 'Huddle' badge but no context about what they might be discussing. Show the most recent activity log entry involving any seated worker as a tiny topic on the table surface. Makes the huddle feel purposeful, not random.

ideas.title: Notification Sound Toggle
ideas.description: The dashboard shows confetti and toasts when workers ship, but there's no audio cue. A subtle chime on task completion and a soft alert on errors would let people keep the tab in the background and still feel connected. Add a mute toggle in the header.

ideas.title: Desk Personal Items That Evolve
ideas.description: Each desk already shows idle props (coffee, duck, plant, books) but they rotate randomly. Give each worker a signature desk item that levels up over time — Carlos gets a growing stack of log printouts, Maya gets an expanding color swatch collection, Dave's headphone cable gets longer. Rewards long-time viewers with continuity.

ideas.title: Office Floor Weather Particles
ideas.description: The vibe weather widget shows text and an icon, but the office floor itself doesn't reflect the weather. Add ambient CSS particles that match: rain drops for slow-day, floating embers for on-fire, subtle sparkles for crushing, snowflakes for after-hours. The floor should FEEL the vibe, not just label it.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Weekly Retro Report
ideas.description: Every Monday, auto-generate a 'weekly retro' view from the activity log: tasks completed, errors encountered, peak vibe moments, MVP worker of the week. Display it as a newspaper-style overlay ('The Bloberto Times'). Turns passive monitoring into a narrative people actually want to read.
